summary: Completed frontend testing of ValuationResults page visual priority hierarchy. All CSS visual hierarchy classes are correctly applied. PRIMARY blocks have blue left border (3px solid #0B4DBB) and enhanced shadow, SECONDARY blocks have standard shadow. Minor backend PDF export issue found (unrelated to visual changes).

minor.issue: PDF export fails with Unicode encoding error - emoji character '⚠' not supported by helveticaB font
minor.error: FPDFUnicodeEncodingException: Character "⚠" at index 0 in text is outside the range of characters supported by the font used
minor.note: This is a pre-existing issue unrelated to the visual hierarchy changes being tested

seed_data_creation: Used existing valuation val_0b6c1d90d0ec for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Visual hierarchy CSS verified working. PRIMARY blocks (Exit Readiness, Deal Killers, Buyer Fit, Optimization) have blue left border + enhanced shadow. SECONDARY blocks (What-If, Exit Scenarios) have standard shadow. PDF export has Unicode emoji issue (pre-existing, unrelated to visual changes).

pdf_export_failure.root_cause: fpdf library using helveticaB font cannot encode Unicode emoji character ⚠ (U+26A0)
pdf_export_failure.fix_approach: Either replace emoji with ASCII text alternative (e.g., 'WARNING:') or configure fpdf to use a Unicode-compatible font
pdf_export_failure.priority: MEDIUM - cosmetic issue, doesn't affect core functionality
